moments_proud: I feel most proud and accomplished when I see the direct impact of our work at Thaddeus Resource Center, especially when we help clients move into better, more stable situations. It’s incredibly rewarding to know that the support we provide is making a real difference in someone’s life. Seeing a client reach a milestone, gain independence, or express gratitude for our help reminds me why the work we do is so important.
moments_frustrated: I feel most challenged or frustrated when a client does not respect my boundaries or consistently pushes against them. It can also be difficult when we are unable to reach clients, especially when we know they need support. These situations can be emotionally draining and make it harder to provide the help we want to give, but I try to stay patient and focused on finding ways to re-engage or set healthy limits.
completion_challenges: Some of the challenges I most often face when trying to complete my tasks include technical issues, unresponsiveness from someone I’m supposed to be working with or receiving support from, and unexpected personal matters that can arise. These challenges can sometimes delay progress, but I do my best to stay flexible and find solutions so I can continue moving forward with my responsibilities.
time_1: Waiting for information, responses, or decisions
resources_valuable: I’m not entirely sure what specific resource or opportunity would be most valuable right now, but I believe that any help is good help. I’m always open to learning and growing, so any support, guidance, or professional development that can improve my skills or help me be more effective in my role would be appreciated.
resources_2keep: The buddy system is working really well and should definitely be maintained. It helps build teamwork and provides support when needed. The way tasks are distributed is also effective, and the resources we have access to make it easier to do our jobs well. These are strengths of the Programs department that should be continued and possibly expanded.
resources_2improve: One thing I would like to see improved in the Programs department is more training on Microsoft Teams, so that everyone feels confident using it for communication and collaboration. I would also like to see a stronger commitment and support directed toward the Mentoring and Housing departments, as they play a vital role in the overall success of our clients and could benefit from more consistent attention and resources.

moments_proud: I feel most proud when I am able to get a client to respond to me/ confide in me when they need assistance. I love having “break-through” moments as it reinforces the idea that the work I am doing is actually very meaningful and important.
moments_frustrated: I feel most frustrated when a client chooses to not accept my assistance or services I have provided. There have been a few clients I have dealt with that desperately need certain resources but do not have the personal motivation to actually make the changes needed in their lives to succeed or make progress.
completion_challenges: I find it very hard to write case notes. I don’t feel I have the proper knowledge to write effective case notes and I have been told there are several ways to do it and am still unsure of which method is the appropriate way.
time_5: Waiting for information, responses, or decisions
resources_valuable: I feel case managers should receive more specific training outside of “buddies” when onboarding. I was originally a navigator and transitioned into a case manager once I joined Thaddeus, so my experience may be different from what is typical. However, there were some tasks that I had to learn on my own and even some that I am still unsure of (i.e. how and where to write and submit case notes). I feel like we need more information on what is expected of us, where to find internal resources, and what tasks we should be doing daily when documentation interactions with clients.
resources_2keep: I enjoy our weekly meetings, and feel the break-out rooms in our Program Department meeting has significantly helped my mental health while working in this role. Being able to discuss how case management impacts my own mental health with other case managers and our mentor has really helped me gain a lot of insight into the role and has helped me feel heard and cared for.
resources_2improve: I feel navigators should get to expand on their roles more. I also feel that case managers, life coaches, care coordinators, and therapists should be more encouraged to partake in some of the program development roles such as the mentoring team and the housing teams. I feel those teams would greatly benefit from input from all of the people who work with clients at Thaddeus.

moments_proud: I feel most proud of my work when clients have "AH-HA" moments about their interpersonal struggles because of how dedicated they are to change and get the most out of therapy.
moments_frustrated: I feel most frustrated when clients don't take therapy seriously or bail on their session. I think the issue with unreliable clients is that we either don't have any effective procedures in place to hold them accountable or the clients don't value our time because they don't take it seriously, which sometimes offends me.
completion_challenges: Sometimes I find it hard to complete tasks when clients are no-shows or waste my time.
time_6: Waiting for information, responses, or decisions

resources_2keep: I like the buddy system and the client presentation weekly meetings.
resources_2improve: I would like the calling and messaging system to be improved. Clients get confused when we call from one number, but text from another.

moments_proud: When I was a case manager helping clients & now that I am a nonprofit leadership and management intern I feel proud and accomplished when I help the other interns in the programs department complete tasks that they were having difficulty with

completion_challenges: Only if I need clarification on exactly how something is supposed to be done, sometimes it takes Kimmie a while to respond, but I just ask someone else like Mary. & for clarification, the reason I put fair above for training questions is because I had to learn how to do everything on my own. There should be a second orientation for just case managers, therapists, whatever your role is... I was so overwhelmed as this was my first position, still in college, that I didn't retain any info from orientation & there should be a day 2 of orientation for role specific training & recordings to go back to if there's questions or something you need to remember. I had a good buddy, Annie. If it wasn't for her, I wouldn't have learned anything that I didn't teach myself. There should also be client relation training for case managers. How to talk to clients, I googled it & practiced on my own.
